milestones.title: M001: Migration
milestones.vision: Tutelo is a web application that gives K-12 classroom teachers a professional tutoring landing page — with scheduling, payments, and booking — in under 7 minutes, at zero upfront cost.

milestones.title: M002: Production Launch
milestones.vision: Tutelo is live on the internet — a real teacher can sign up, publish a page, and receive a booking request from a real parent, with all payment and notification infrastructure working in production.
success_criteria: The app is deployed and accessible at a public URL (Vercel)
Supabase remote project has all 6 migrations applied with RLS active
Stripe webhooks are configured and verified with real signing secrets
Email notifications are delivered to real email addresses via Resend
A teacher can complete the full onboarding flow on the live URL
No raw error messages or stack traces are visible to end users
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02

Produces:
- Live Vercel URL with all env vars configured
- Supabase remote project with all 6 migrations applied and RLS active
- Stripe webhook endpoints registered in Stripe Dashboard
- Resend domain verified and API key active

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S02 → S03

Produces:
- Verified auth flow (signup, login, session persistence)
- Verified booking flow (request creation, DB record, email sent)
- Verified Stripe webhook receipt (test event processed)
- List of any integration issues discovered and fixed

Consumes:
- Live Vercel deployment from S01

milestones.title: M003: Landing Page & Polish
milestones.vision: Tutelo looks and feels like a real product — a polished marketing landing page at tutelo.app/, smooth animations throughout, mobile-friendly dashboard, and professional share previews. Teachers who visit the URL for the first time are impressed; those who sign up feel the quality at every step.
success_criteria: tutelo.app/ displays a branded marketing landing page (not the Next.js default)
The landing page communicates: professional identity, zero risk, how it works, problem/solution contrast, and shareable slug links
An interactive teacher page mock is visible on the landing page

Framer Motion animations are active on: landing page (scroll reveals), onboarding (step transitions), dashboard (card/list animations), teacher profile (section fades), and micro-interactions (buttons, toggles, focus states)
Dashboard has a functional bottom tab bar on mobile viewports (375px)
Teacher /[slug] links generate personalized OG previews (name, photo, subjects)
New teacher signups have social_email auto-populated from their auth email
`npm run build` succeeds with no regressions
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:

milestones.title: M004: Availability & Scheduling Overhaul
milestones.vision: Teachers control their schedule with 5-minute precision, override specific dates, and cancel sessions with a single click — replacing the rigid 1-hour weekly grid with a flexible, real-life-ready availability system.
success_criteria: A teacher can set availability as time ranges (e.g., 3:30–4:45 PM) in 5-minute increments on any day of the week
A teacher can set availability for a specific future date that overrides their recurring weekly pattern
When an override exists for a date, the parent-facing booking calendar shows only the override slots (not recurring)
An existing teacher's current 1-hour recurring availability is preserved after migration with no data loss
The parent-facing booking calendar displays bookable time slots derived from the new granularity
A teacher can cancel an upcoming confirmed session from their dashboard, triggering an immediate cancellation email to the parent
`npm run build` succeeds with zero errors
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02

Produces:
- `availability` table unchanged structurally (TIME columns already support 5-min values)
- Redesigned `AvailabilityEditor.tsx` with time-range picker paradigm (start/end dropdowns per day, multiple windows per day)
- `updateAvailability` server action accepting `{ day_of_week, start_time, end_time }[]` with 5-min-aligned HH:MM values
- Overlap validation utility function (sort-and-compare on start_time per day)
- 5-minute time options generator (288 HH:MM strings)

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03

Produces:
- Recurring availability stored as time ranges (not individual hour blocks) — `start_time` and `end_time` at 5-min granularity
- Same `availability` table schema — downstream queries unchanged structurally

### S02 → S03

Produces:
- `availability_overrides` table: `(id, teacher_id, specific_date, start_time, end_time, created_at)` with `UNIQUE(teacher_id, specific_date, start_time)`
- Override save/delete server actions following existing `getClaims()` auth pattern
- Override-wins-recurring precedence logic: "if any override row exists for date X, use only override rows; ignore recurring for that date"

Consumes:
- S01: time-range editor pattern, overlap validation, 5-min time options generator

### S04 (independent)

Produces:
- "Cancel Session" button on `ConfirmedSessionCard`
- `cancelSession` server action: sets booking status to `cancelled`, calls `sendCancellationEmail(bookingId)`, revalidates paths

Consumes:
- Existing `sendCancellationEmail` in `src/lib/email.ts`
- Existing `ConfirmedSessionCard` component and `startTransition` + server action pattern

milestones.title: M005: Trust & Communication
milestones.vision: Parents receive text message reminders and instant cancellation alerts, and teachers who verify their school email earn a "Verified Teacher" badge — building real trust and reducing no-shows.
success_criteria: Teacher can add their phone number during onboarding or in account settings, with explicit SMS opt-in consent
Parent can provide a phone number with SMS opt-in on the booking form
Both teacher and parent receive an SMS reminder 24 hours before a session (when opted in)
When a teacher cancels last-minute, the parent receives an SMS within the same request (not a later cron)
A teacher can submit their school email address, receive a verification link, click it, and see their profile badge change to "Verified Teacher"
An unverified teacher's profile does NOT show the "Verified Teacher" badge (fixing the current hardcoded badge)
All SMS sends are gated on `phone_number IS NOT NULL AND sms_opt_in = true` — never sent without explicit consent
`npm run build` passes with zero errors after all slices
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02

Produces:

- DB columns: `teachers.phone_number TEXT`, `teachers.sms_opt_in BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE`, `bookings.parent_phone TEXT`, `bookings.parent_sms_opt_in BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE`
- `TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID`, `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`, `TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER` env vars expected
- Phone number validation using `libphonenumber-js` integrated into Zod schemas
- `src/app/api/cron/session-reminders/route.ts` extended to send SMS alongside email
- `src/actions/bookings.ts` `cancelSession` extended to call `sendSmsCancellation`

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03

Produces:
- DB column: `teachers.verified_at TIMESTAMPTZ` (nullable — null means unverified) — included in S01 migration

Consumes:

### S02 → (terminal)

Produces:

### S03 → (terminal)

Produces:

- If S01 hasn't run yet, S03 must include or depend on the migration — but since both can run independently, S03 should check that migration 0008 exists and create it if not

milestones.title: Growth Tools
milestones.vision: Give teachers zero-friction promotion tools — QR codes for print materials, pre-written announcement templates for digital sharing, and verified social preview cards — so they can actively promote their Tutelo pages without decision fatigue.
success_criteria: Teacher can download a QR code PNG that scans to their tutelo.app/[slug] URL
Teacher can download a styled mini-flyer with QR code, name, subjects, rate, and CTA
Teacher can copy at least 4 pre-written announcement templates interpolated with their profile data
Copy-to-clipboard works with confirmation micro-interaction on all templates
Teacher's Tutelo link unfurls into a professional preview card in iMessage, WhatsApp, and Facebook
Build passes with no regressions on existing test suite

key_risks.risk: OG image rendering varies by platform
key_risks.whyItMatters: WhatsApp and iMessage have different cache behaviors, image size requirements, and refresh intervals — may need platform-specific fixes

key_risks.risk: Mini-flyer client-side rendering quality
key_risks.whyItMatters: html2canvas can have font rendering issues — may need server-side ImageResponse approach for consistent quality

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: OG image renders correctly in at least 3 major platforms with no broken layouts or missing images
Mini-flyer generates with correct teacher data, readable QR code, and print-quality resolution

milestones.verification_contract: Unit tests for QR URL generation, template interpolation, copy-to-clipboard. Build passes. No regressions on existing 411+ tests.
milestones.verification_integration: OG image verified rendering in iMessage, WhatsApp, Facebook via real URL testing.
milestones.verification_operational: None — no new services or crons.
milestones.verification_uat: Teacher downloads QR code and scans it with phone camera. Teacher prints flyer and verifies readability. Teacher copies template and pastes into email client.
definition_of_done: All three growth tools accessible from teacher dashboard
QR code encodes correct slug URL and downloads as high-res PNG
Mini-flyer renders with teacher data and is print-ready quality
At least 4 announcement templates with working copy-to-clipboard
OG image verified rendering in at least 3 major platforms
Build passes, no regressions on existing test suite
All new components follow dashboard UI patterns (shadcn/ui, 4pt grid, max 24px)
milestones.requirement_coverage: Covers: QR-01, QR-02, SWIPE-01, SWIPE-02, OG-01. Partially covers: none. Leaves for later: all M007-M010 requirements. Orphan risks: none.

milestones.vision: Teachers can cap their student load and offer variable pricing per session type. When at capacity, parents join a waitlist and get notified when a spot opens. Teachers who define session types (e.g. "SAT Prep $60 / 90 min") see parents charged the right price through Stripe. Teachers who never create session types continue working exactly as before.
success_criteria: A teacher with capacity_limit=3 and 3 active students → profile page shows 'at capacity' + waitlist form; a parent joins waitlist, a booking is cancelled, waitlisted parents receive email with booking link, next parent can book successfully
A teacher with 2 session types → parent visits profile, selects 'SAT Prep $60 / 90 min', books a 90-min slot, Stripe PaymentIntent is created for $60 (not computed from hourly_rate × duration)
A teacher with no session types and no capacity limit → booking flow works exactly as today (subject selector, hourly_rate proration, no capacity check)
Migration 0011 applies cleanly to existing schema with no destructive changes
All new features have unit tests; npm run build passes; npx tsc --noEmit exits 0

key_risks.risk: Session type locked duration + slot generation interaction
key_risks.whyItMatters: A 90-min session type must filter out 30-min slots where 90 minutes don't fit within the availability window. Requires a filter pass after slot generation without changing SLOT_DURATION_MS (which would break hourly_rate path).

key_risks.risk: BookingCalendar state machine complexity
key_risks.whyItMatters: Already a 400-line 6-step client component. Adding session type selector, duration override, and conditional pricing display must not break existing deferred or direct booking paths.

key_risks.risk: Two pricing paths coexisting in create-intent
key_risks.whyItMatters: Session-type flat price vs hourly_rate proration must not interfere. Security: must validate session_type_id belongs to the teacher to prevent price substitution.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Unit test: 3-hour availability window with 90-min session type produces only valid start slots (those where start + 90 min ≤ window end)
Build passes with both session-type and hourly_rate paths; teacher with no session types sees unchanged booking flow
Unit tests prove checkAndNotifyWaitlist fires on cancellation, rechecks capacity, and only emails unnotified entries

milestones.verification_contract: npx tsc --noEmit exits 0; npm run build passes; all new unit tests pass via npx vitest run
milestones.verification_integration: Booking flow end-to-end: session-type pricing through Stripe PaymentIntent; waitlist email fires on capacity change; profile page toggles between booking calendar and at-capacity state based on real DB counts
milestones.verification_operational: Existing teachers with no session types and no capacity limit experience zero behavior change after migration
milestones.verification_uat: Teacher sets capacity → reaches limit → profile shows at-capacity → parent joins waitlist → teacher cancels a booking → waitlisted parents get email → parent books via email link. Teacher creates session types → parent selects type → sees correct price → Stripe PI matches.
definition_of_done: Migration 0011 applies cleanly with capacity_limit, waitlist, and session_types tables
Capacity settings UI in dashboard allows setting/clearing limit
Profile page shows at-capacity state with waitlist form when limit reached
Teacher waitlist dashboard page with view/remove functionality
Waitlist notification emails fire on cancellation when capacity frees up
Session types CRUD in dashboard settings
Booking form shows session type selector when types exist, with locked duration slot filtering
create-intent uses flat session-type price when session_type_id present
Teachers without session types see unchanged booking flow (hourly_rate fallback)
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0, npm run build passes, all tests pass
milestones.requirement_coverage: CAP-01 → S01 (primary), CAP-02 → S01 (primary) + S02 (supporting, waitlist form on at-capacity page), WAIT-01 → S01 (primary, waitlist signup form), WAIT-02 → S02 (primary), WAIT-03 → S02 (primary), SESS-01 → S03 (primary), SESS-02 → S03 (primary), SESS-03 → S03 (primary), SESS-04 → S03 (primary). All 9 M007 Active requirements are mapped. No orphaned requirements.

milestones.vision: Tutelo goes from invite-only 1:1 links to a publicly discoverable directory that Google indexes. Teachers get page-view and conversion funnel analytics so they can see if their promotion efforts are working. Parents searching "math tutor in Chicago" can find Tutelo organically.
success_criteria: /tutors page lists published teachers with working subject, grade-level, location, and price filters via URL params
Full-text search across name, school, subjects, bio returns relevant results using Postgres tsvector + GIN index
SEO category pages (/tutors/math, /tutors/chicago) render with correct meta tags and are statically generated
XML sitemap at /sitemap.xml includes all published teacher /[slug] URLs plus directory and category pages
Page views tracked on /[slug] pages with bot filtering; stored in page_views table
Dashboard analytics section shows: total views, booking form opens, completed bookings, conversion rate

key_risks.risk: tsvector/GIN index migration on teachers table could be slow on a large dataset
key_risks.whyItMatters: Migration runs on production — if it locks the table, teacher profile pages go down during deploy

key_risks.risk: Bot filtering for page views is imprecise
key_risks.whyItMatters: Inflated counts mislead teachers about their actual reach; must at least filter Googlebot/common crawlers

key_risks.risk: Static generation of category pages may be stale if teachers publish/unpublish frequently
key_risks.whyItMatters: ISR or dynamic rendering tradeoff — stale pages hurt discoverability; real-time is expensive

key_risks.risk: SEO duplicate content: category pages share teacher cards with /tutors directory
key_risks.whyItMatters: Google may penalize duplicate content if canonical tags and unique page titles are not set correctly

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Migration uses CONCURRENTLY for GIN index creation (no table lock); local migration runs clean on test DB
User-agent check filters top-10 crawlers; middleware logs raw vs filtered counts in dev for manual verification
Pages use revalidate=3600 (ISR) so they refresh within 1 hour of teacher publishing; not real-time but acceptable
Each category page has unique title/description meta tags; canonical tag points to itself (not /tutors)

milestones.verification_contract: All TypeScript compiles clean (npx tsc --noEmit). npm run build passes. Directory page renders with filters. Full-text search returns results for at least one known teacher. Category pages render with correct meta. /sitemap.xml returns valid XML with at least one teacher URL. Page views increment on /[slug] visits. Dashboard analytics section renders without error.
milestones.verification_integration: Filters + search work together (combined query). Category page links appear in sitemap. Analytics funnel connects page_views → booking_form_opens (if tracked) → bookings table.
milestones.verification_operational: Page view tracking does not add measurable latency to /[slug] page loads. GIN index migration runs without downtime on production schema.
milestones.verification_uat: Teacher opens dashboard, sees analytics section with view count. Parent visits /tutors, filters by subject, sees relevant teachers. Parent uses search bar, sees relevant results. /sitemap.xml is reachable and parseable by Google Search Console.
definition_of_done: All 6 success criteria passing
tsc --noEmit clean
npm run build passes
At least 10 unit tests covering filter query logic and analytics tracking
All active M008 requirements (DIR-01, DIR-02, DIR-03, SEO-03, SEO-04, ANALYTICS-01, ANALYTICS-02) have validation evidence
milestones.requirement_coverage: DIR-01 (directory page + filters) → S01. DIR-02 (filter mechanics) → S01. DIR-03 (full-text search) → S02. SEO-03 (sitemap) → S03. SEO-04 (category pages) → S03. ANALYTICS-01 (page view tracking) → S04. ANALYTICS-02 (dashboard analytics) → S04.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
| Layer | In | Out |
|---|---|---|
| Directory UI | /tutors page, filter sidebar, teacher cards, URL param sync | Map-based discovery, teacher ranking algorithm |
| Search | tsvector full-text search, GIN index migration | Algolia, Meilisearch, any external search |
| SEO | Category pages, sitemap.xml, canonical tags, meta per page | Structured data (JSON-LD), Google Search Console API |
| Analytics | page_views table, bot filter, dashboard funnel display | Real-time analytics, historical export, heatmaps |
| Scope | Only published (is_published=true) teachers shown | Admin tools, teacher management |

milestones.title: Recurring Sessions
milestones.vision: Parents can set up recurring tutoring schedules (weekly/biweekly for N weeks). The system creates individual booking rows per session, handles per-session payment via saved cards with auto-charging 24h before each session, and lets both teacher and parent cancel single sessions or the remaining series.
success_criteria: Parent can select a recurring schedule (weekly/biweekly, N weeks) in the booking flow and see which dates were skipped due to conflicts
Individual booking rows are created per session, linked by recurring_schedule_id
First session is authorized via existing PaymentIntent flow; subsequent sessions are auto-charged 24h before via saved card
Teacher sees recurring sessions in dashboard with series badge and can cancel one or remaining series
Parent can cancel individual sessions or remaining series via secure email link
Cron successfully charges saved cards for upcoming recurring sessions with failure handling and notifications

key_risks.risk: Stripe SetupIntent + saved payment method flow is new surface area for Tutelo
key_risks.whyItMatters: Card saving, 3D Secure re-auth, and auto-charging have different failure modes than the existing authorize-then-capture flow. If this doesn't work, the entire recurring payment model breaks.

key_risks.risk: Cron payment failure handling must be bulletproof
key_risks.whyItMatters: A failed charge 24h before a session means the session shouldn't proceed as if paid. Need clear failure states, notifications, and a path for the parent to retry or the booking to be cancelled.

key_risks.risk: Multi-date availability conflict checking across N future dates
key_risks.whyItMatters: Must check recurring slots + overrides + existing bookings for each date in the series. Overrides can close a day, new bookings can appear. Performance and correctness both matter.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: SetupIntent saves card; cron creates PaymentIntent from saved card with correct amount, destination charge, and application fee. Unit tests for the charge logic.
Failed charge updates booking status to 'payment_failed'; notifications sent to both parties. Unit tests for failure path.

Conflict checker correctly identifies unavailable dates across recurring slots, overrides, and existing bookings. Partial series creation with skipped-date summary. Unit tests for all conflict scenarios.

milestones.verification_contract: tsc --noEmit clean. npm run build passes. Unit tests for: recurring schedule creation, multi-date conflict detection, cron charge logic, cancellation (single + series), and parent self-service cancel flow. All RECUR-01 through RECUR-09 requirements have validation evidence.
milestones.verification_integration: Stripe Customer + SetupIntent saves card. Cron creates PaymentIntent from saved card. cancelSession handles recurring_schedule_id for series cancellation. Parent cancel page works via secure token.
milestones.verification_operational: Daily cron charges recurring sessions within Vercel Hobby cron limits. Failed charges don't block or crash the cron — per-booking error handling.
milestones.verification_uat: Parent completes recurring booking flow end-to-end. Teacher sees series badge in dashboard. Teacher cancels one session and remaining series. Parent cancels via email link. Cron auto-charges upcoming session.
definition_of_done: All 6 success criteria passing
tsc --noEmit clean
npm run build passes
Unit tests for conflict detection, series creation, cron charging, cancellation, and parent self-service
All RECUR-01 through RECUR-09 requirements have validation evidence
Recurring booking flow works end-to-end (booking → card saved → cron charges → cancellation)
milestones.requirement_coverage: RECUR-01 (recurring schedule selection) → S01. RECUR-02 (auto-create booking rows) → S01. RECUR-03 (per-session payment) → S02. RECUR-04 (cancel individual/series) → S03. RECUR-05 (availability + double-booking prevention) → S01. RECUR-06 (saved card via SetupIntent) → S02. RECUR-07 (cron auto-charge) → S02. RECUR-08 (parent self-service cancel) → S03. RECUR-09 (dashboard series badge) → S03.

milestones.vision: Transform Tutelo's parent side from anonymous guest-booking into a full account experience — login-required dashboard with multi-child management, saved payment cards, real-time teacher-parent messaging — and give the platform operator a read-only admin dashboard with metrics and activity feed. Verify Google SSO end-to-end.
success_criteria: Parent can log in, see a dashboard with children, booking history, saved card, and message threads
Parent can add a child and book a session selecting that child from a dropdown instead of typing a name
Parent's card is auto-saved on first Stripe booking; subsequent bookings use saved card in one click
Teacher and parent can exchange text messages in real-time within the app, with email notifications
Google SSO works end-to-end for both teacher and parent accounts
Teacher who signed in via Google can still verify school email for the Verified badge
Platform operator visits /admin and sees teacher count, booking volume, revenue, and recent activity
All existing booking flows (guest, direct, recurring) continue working unchanged

key_risks.risk: Supabase Realtime has never been used in this project
key_risks.whyItMatters: Subscription stability, RLS interaction, and reconnection behavior are unproven — messaging depends entirely on this

key_risks.risk: Parent dashboard is a new route group coexisting with teacher dashboard
key_risks.whyItMatters: Dual-role users (teacher + parent) need a coherent navigation experience; auth callback routing must handle both roles

key_risks.risk: Google OAuth provider may not be configured in Supabase dashboard
key_risks.whyItMatters: Configuration is external and untestable in unit tests — end-to-end verification requires manual setup

key_risks.risk: Saved card reuse requires parent-level Stripe Customer distinct from per-schedule Customers
key_risks.whyItMatters: Existing recurring schedule Customers are per-schedule; parent-level Customer is additive but must not break existing flows

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Live Realtime subscription receives INSERT events on messages table, filtered by conversation_id, with RLS enforced
Parent can log in, see /parent dashboard, and teacher dashboard still works — auth callback routes correctly
Full OAuth flow from button click through callback to dashboard landing, for both new and returning users
Parent-level Customer stores card, card is reusable across teacher bookings, existing recurring Customers unaffected

milestones.verification_contract: Unit tests for children CRUD, saved card flow, messaging thread/send, admin queries; tsc --noEmit clean; npm run build passes

milestones.vision: Raise every user-facing surface from functional MVP to modern SaaS premium. The teacher profile page (conversion surface), booking flow, mobile navigation, both dashboards, and landing page all get intentional design treatment. No surface should look like a generic template or feel clunky to use.
success_criteria: Teacher /[slug] profile page looks polished and premium — hero, credentials, about, reviews all have intentional design
Booking flow from date → time → form → payment feels smooth, not heavy or confusing
Mobile bottom nav shows labeled primary tabs with a More menu — users can navigate without guessing
Teacher dashboard pages (11 pages) have premium card treatments, visual hierarchy, and intentional spacing
Parent dashboard pages (5 pages) match the same visual standard as teacher dashboard
Landing page is tightened — sharper typography, more intentional spacing
Typography, spacing, and component patterns are consistent across every surface
No surface looks like a generic shadcn/ui template with default styling
All 474+ existing tests pass with zero regressions
tsc --noEmit clean and next build succeeds

key_risks.risk: BookingCalendar 935-line monolith decomposition
key_risks.whyItMatters: Three booking paths (deferred, direct, recurring) plus session types, child selector, recurring options, and inline auth all live in one component. Decomposing while preserving all paths risks functional regressions.

key_risks.risk: Scope creep — polish is unbounded
key_risks.whyItMatters: Each surface could absorb infinite attention. Without clear 'done' criteria per slice, this milestone could expand indefinitely.

key_risks.risk: Mobile nav More menu interaction pattern
key_risks.whyItMatters: The slide-up sheet needs to feel native and smooth, not janky. Animation timing and backdrop behavior matter.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: All 3 booking paths work end-to-end after decomposition — deferred booking, direct payment, recurring payment. Existing booking tests pass.
Each slice has explicit 'done' criteria in its plan. Browser verification against running app confirms visual outcomes.
More menu opens smoothly, shows all remaining items with labels, closes on tap-outside. Works at 375px and 390px viewports.

milestones.verification_contract: All 474+ existing tests pass. tsc --noEmit clean. next build succeeds. No functional regressions in any booking path.
milestones.verification_integration: Visual consistency across all surfaces verified via browser at desktop and mobile viewports.
milestones.verification_uat: User can navigate the teacher profile page, complete a mock booking flow, navigate the mobile dashboard, and browse all dashboard pages — everything feels cohesive and premium.

All 11 teacher dashboard pages and 5 parent dashboard pages polished
Landing page tightened

No surface looks like a generic template

milestones.title: Performance & Delivery Efficiency
milestones.vision: Serve public pages from Vercel's CDN instead of dynamic SSR, extend query caching across all dashboard pages, and trim JS bundle weight — all within Vercel Hobby plan constraints. The profile page load goes from ~800ms dynamic SSR to ~50ms CDN-cached. Dashboard navigation feels instant on re-visit.
success_criteria: All profile page requests to /[slug] return x-vercel-cache: HIT header post-deploy
Dashboard navigation within 30 seconds shows cached query results instantly
npm run build shows no motion library chunks in dashboard route bundles
All 4 ISR + caching slices pass their respective UAT without Vercel plan limit breaches

milestones.title: Codebase Cohesion & Observability
milestones.vision: Bring the codebase from 'features work' to 'codebase is maintainable and observable.' Fix all broken tests, integrate Sentry error tracking, audit and resolve all test stubs, and rebuild the full capability contract. No new features, no UI changes, no schema migrations.
success_criteria: npx vitest run → 0 failures, 0 orphaned todo stubs
npx tsc --noEmit → clean
npx next build → succeeds
Sentry captures errors from both client error boundaries and server-side catch blocks
REQUIREMENTS.md documents all 124+ validated capabilities from M001–M012

key_risks.risk: Test stub audit effort is variable
key_risks.whyItMatters: Some stubs may represent real coverage gaps needing non-trivial mocking (onboarding wizard, mark-complete flow). May need to timebox.

key_risks.risk: Sentry + Next.js 16 SDK compatibility
key_risks.whyItMatters: Sentry's Next.js SDK may have quirks with Next.js 16.1.6. Need to verify instrumentation wizard works with current setup.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Each stub audited and resolved — deleted if redundant, converted if gap. Any stubs too expensive to convert are documented and tracked.
Sentry SDK initializes, captures a test error, and source maps resolve in dev. No build regressions.

milestones.verification_contract: All tests pass (npx vitest run → 0 failures, 0 todos), tsc clean, build succeeds
milestones.verification_integration: Sentry receives errors from both client React error boundaries and server-side catch blocks in dev environment
milestones.verification_operational: Sentry dashboard shows captured errors with stack traces and source maps after deliberate error trigger
milestones.verification_uat: Trigger a test error in production and confirm it appears in Sentry dashboard with full context
definition_of_done: All 14 broken tests fixed and passing
Sentry SDK integrated — client + server + source maps
Error boundaries report to Sentry before rendering fallback
All 46 catch blocks audited — none silently swallow errors
All 45 todo stubs resolved (deleted or converted)
REQUIREMENTS.md contains full 124+ validated capabilities with traceability
npx vitest run → 0 failures, 0 todos
npx tsc --noEmit → clean
npx next build → succeeds

### S02 (independent)

milestones.title: Dashboard Mobile Performance
milestones.vision: Make the teacher dashboard feel fast on mobile by eliminating the server-side auth waterfall that blocks rendering, and trimming avoidable JS from the shared bundle. The layout shell (sidebar, bottom nav, skeleton) should appear instantly while page data streams in. Secondary: cut ~800K of shared JS (Zod, Sentry, motion) that bloats every page even though most routes don't use it.
success_criteria: Dashboard layout shell (sidebar + bottom nav + skeleton) renders before page data query completes
Dashboard TTFB under 200ms on warm cache (currently blocked by sequential auth + data queries)
Zod removed from client bundle (saves 296K from shared chunks)
Sentry client SDK lazy-loaded after hydration (saves ~211K from critical path)
All 490 tests still pass
No visual regressions on dashboard, login, or onboarding

key_risks.risk: Supabase middleware may conflict with existing auth-cache pattern
key_risks.whyItMatters: auth-cache uses getClaims() for speed — middleware must complement it, not duplicate work

key_risks.risk: Middleware edge runtime limits available Supabase SDK features
key_risks.whyItMatters: Edge runtime can't use Node.js APIs — need @supabase/ssr createServerClient with cookie forwarding

key_risks.risk: Lazy Sentry may miss errors during initial hydration
key_risks.whyItMatters: Errors thrown before Sentry loads would be queued, not lost, but timing matters

definition_of_done: npx next build succeeds
npx vitest run: 0 failures
Dashboard layout renders skeleton immediately on navigation (visual verification)
Unauthenticated /dashboard requests redirect to /login via middleware (not layout)

milestones.title: Pre-Launch Hardening
milestones.vision: Close the remaining launch gaps before MVP goes live: tighten email deliverability with DMARC and bounce/complaint tracking, confirm cron jobs are actually running in production, protect public endpoints from abuse, and lock down the critical booking flow behind an automated end-to-end test.
success_criteria: DMARC record live on tutelo.app in at least `p=none` monitor mode
Resend webhook endpoint receives bounce/complaint events and logs them to Sentry
All Vercel cron routes (session-reminders, auto-cancel, stripe-reminders, recurring-charges) verified as scheduled and invocable with CRON_SECRET auth
Public endpoints (/api/waitlist, /api/track-view, /api/verify-email) rate-limited to prevent abuse
Playwright E2E test covers the full booking happy path (profile → book → Stripe test card → cancel → email assertions) and runs green in CI

key_risks.risk: Vercel cron jobs may not be enabled on current plan tier
key_risks.whyItMatters: If crons aren't running, session reminders, auto-cancel, and recurring charges all silently fail — meaning users don't get reminders, no-show bookings stay on calendars, and recurring sessions never charge. Catching this before launch is critical.

key_risks.risk: Rate limiting implementation may need a KV store (Upstash/Vercel KV) that requires new env vars and cost
key_risks.whyItMatters: In-memory rate limiting doesn't work across Vercel serverless instances. We may need to pick between Upstash Redis (has free tier), Vercel KV (bundled), or header-based throttling via Vercel's built-in WAF.

key_risks.risk: Playwright E2E test requires a dedicated test parent account, test teacher profile, and Stripe test mode — setup may be brittle
key_risks.whyItMatters: If the test assumes specific DB state that drifts, it becomes a maintenance burden. Need to seed state fresh each run or use fixtures.

key_risks.risk: DMARC in enforcement mode (p=quarantine/reject) could break legitimate sends if SPF/DKIM alignment is off
key_risks.whyItMatters: Starting with p=none is safe (monitor only), but we need to explicitly document that and not bump to quarantine until we've seen at least a week of clean reports.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Cron jobs may not be scheduled
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Each cron route is listed in Vercel dashboard with an active schedule and has been manually invoked once with a 200 response in logs

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Rate limiting needs a distributed store
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Burst traffic (50 requests in 10s from single IP) returns 429 on /api/waitlist; legitimate single requests return 201

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Booking flow may have a regression
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Playwright test signs up a parent, books a session, completes Stripe test card payment, confirms booking email landed, cancels, confirms cancellation email — all assertions green

milestones.verification_contract: Each slice ships with a verification command that must pass before the slice is closed. S01: `dig TXT _dmarc.tutelo.app` returns the expected record + webhook curl returns 200 + Sentry receives test event. S02: `curl` against each cron route with valid CRON_SECRET returns 200 + Vercel logs show scheduled invocations. S03: Load test script triggers rate limit. S04: Vitest unit tests for new middleware + manual burst test. S05: `npx playwright test booking-e2e` passes.
milestones.verification_integration: S01 proves email deliverability is monitored. S02 proves background jobs run. S03 provides the rate-limiting primitive. S04 proves the primitive is wired to public endpoints. S05 proves the full user journey works end-to-end against a real deployment.
milestones.verification_operational: Every deployed change is visible in Sentry (DMARC reports → Sentry, webhook failures → Sentry, rate-limit triggers → Sentry). Cron job failures already go to Sentry via the `.catch` wrappers. E2E test failures block CI.
milestones.verification_uat: S05 is itself the UAT \u2014 if the Playwright test passes against production (or a preview deploy), the booking flow is proven to work for a real user.
definition_of_done: DMARC TXT record present on _dmarc.tutelo.app and validated via dig
Resend webhook secret set in Vercel env, endpoint returns 200 on POST, test event appears in Sentry
Cron routes listed in Vercel dashboard with correct schedules, manually triggered once each and verified in logs
Rate limiting middleware rejects burst traffic on public endpoints (verified by script)
Playwright E2E test passes locally and in CI, covers success + cancellation paths
All changes deployed to production, no regressions in existing test suite
milestones.requirement_coverage: Advances R-Deliverability, R-Observability (already validated for app errors via M013, extended here to email + cron), R-AbusePrevention (new), R-BookingFlowContract (new).
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
**S01 → S02:** DMARC record is independent; webhook endpoint lands first. Cron verification depends on nothing.
**S02 → S03:** Cron verification is standalone.
**S03 → S04:** S03 builds the rate-limit primitive (middleware + store choice); S04 wires it to specific routes.
**S04 → S05:** Rate limiting must not break the E2E test \u2014 test IP should be allowlisted or use generous limits.
**S05 stands alone:** E2E test is the final proof before launch.

slices.title: Foundation
slices.demo: Auth, teacher onboarding wizard, public profile page, availability, page visibility — a teacher can sign up, complete onboarding, and publish a live landing page at their vanity URL with no payment setup required.
slices.goal: Bootstrap the Tutelo Next.

slices.title: Booking Requests
slices.demo: Pre-payment booking loop — parent submits booking request, teacher receives "money waiting" email, accepts or declines from dashboard. Atomic booking creation with DB-level double-booking prevention.
slices.goal: Wire the existing BookingCalendar stub into a real booking flow: Postgres atomic insert function, Zod validation, Server Action, and updated BookingCalendar with subject dropdown and inline success/error states.

slices.title: Stripe Connect Deferred Payment
slices.demo: Revenue path — Stripe Connect Express onboarding, deferred Checkout session creation for waiting parents, payment capture with 7% platform fee, 48hr auto-cancel cron, follow-up email escalation.
slices.goal: Install the Stripe SDK, build the /dashboard/connect-stripe page with a single "Connect with Stripe" Server Action, and wire both webhook endpoints (platform + connected-account).

slices.title: Direct Booking Parent Account
slices.demo: Direct booking flow for Stripe-connected teachers (slot → auth → payment in one session), parent /account page with booking history and rebook, 24hr session reminder cron.
slices.goal: Lay the foundation for Phase 4: run the DB migration that adds `reminder_sent_at` to bookings, then scaffold all 7 Wave-0 test files as green `it.

slices.title: Dashboard Reviews
slices.demo: Teacher dashboard (overview stats, sessions history, student list), token-based review flow (mark complete → email → submit review), reviews displayed on public profile.
slices.goal: Lay the schema foundation and test scaffold for Phase 5.

slices.title: Deploy & Configure
slices.demo: The app is live on Vercel with Supabase, Stripe, and Resend configured. All routes load without errors.
slices.goal: Deploy Tutelo to Vercel and configure all external services (Supabase remote, Stripe, Resend) so every route loads without errors.

slices.title: Integration Verification
slices.demo: Auth, booking, Stripe webhooks, and email delivery are verified working on the live URL. Any broken integrations are fixed.
slices.goal: Verify all critical user flows work end-to-end on the live URL and fix any integration issues discovered.

slices.title: Production Hardening
slices.demo: Error boundaries catch unexpected failures gracefully, environment is documented for the founder, and the app is ready for real teachers.
slices.goal: Add error boundaries so users never see raw stack traces, document the production environment for the founder, and ensure the app is ready for real teachers.

slices.title: Brand Identity & Landing Page
slices.demo: Visit tutelo.app/ and see a polished marketing landing page with brand colors, logo, hero, how-it-works, problem/solution, interactive teacher page mock, and "Start your page" CTA. OG meta tags present. Brand palette applied globally.
slices.goal: Replace the Next.js default homepage with a polished marketing landing page at tutelo.app/ using brand identity (#3b4d3e sage green, #f6f5f0 warm off-white, Tutelo logo). Apply brand palette globally without breaking teacher accent colors. Sections: hero, how-it-works, problem/solution, interactive teacher mock, slug showcase, and CTA. OG meta tags present. Logo integrated into Sidebar.

slices.title: Animation System & App-Wide Polish
slices.demo: Navigate the app and see smooth animations everywhere — landing page scroll reveals, page transitions, onboarding step slides, dashboard card animations, profile section fades, and micro-interactions on buttons/toggles/forms.
slices.goal: Every major surface of the Tutelo app has smooth, subtle animations — landing page scroll reveals, page transitions, onboarding step slides, dashboard card staggers, profile section fades, and micro-interactions on interactive elements.

slices.title: Mobile Dashboard & Responsive Polish
slices.demo: Open the dashboard on a phone-sized viewport and navigate via a bottom tab bar. All dashboard pages accessible. Logo visible in mobile header.
slices.goal: Dashboard is fully navigable on mobile viewports via a bottom tab bar, with the Tutelo logo visible in a mobile header.

slices.title: OG Tags, Email Fix & Deploy
slices.demo: Share a teacher's tutelo.app/ms-johnson link and see a personalized preview. Sign up as a new teacher and social_email is auto-populated. Everything deployed to production.
slices.goal: Teacher `/[slug]` links produce personalized OG previews (name, photo, subjects), new teacher signups auto-populate `social_email`, and the full M003 milestone is deployed to production.

slices.title: Recurring Availability Editor with 5-Min Granularity
slices.demo: A teacher can open the redesigned availability editor, set time ranges like 3:30–4:45 PM for each day of the week, save, and see them persisted — replacing the 1-hour block grid entirely.
slices.goal: Replace the 1-hour toggle grid availability editor with a time-range picker paradigm supporting 5-minute granularity, multiple windows per day, and round-trip persistence through the existing `availability` table.

slices.title: Per-Date Override Availability
slices.demo: A teacher can switch to a "Specific Dates" tab in the editor, pick a future date, set custom time ranges for that date, and save. The teacher's public profile page shows override availability for that date instead of the recurring pattern.
slices.goal: A teacher can set custom availability for specific future dates (overriding their recurring weekly pattern), and a visitor sees override availability on the teacher's public profile for those dates.

slices.title: Booking Calendar Integration
slices.demo: A parent viewing a teacher's page sees bookable time slots that reflect both recurring availability and per-date overrides with correct precedence — override wins for dates where overrides exist.
slices.goal: The parent-facing booking calendar presents bookable 30-minute time slots expanded from availability windows, with correct override-wins-recurring precedence, and payment amounts are prorated to session duration.

slices.title: Last-Minute Session Cancellation
slices.demo: A teacher can click "Cancel Session" on a confirmed session card in their dashboard, which cancels the booking and sends an immediate cancellation email to the parent.
slices.goal: A teacher can cancel a confirmed session from their dashboard, voiding the Stripe payment authorization and sending an immediate cancellation email to the parent.

slices.title: SMS Infrastructure & Teacher Phone Collection
slices.demo: Teacher can add phone number with SMS opt-in during onboarding (WizardStep1) and in account settings; `src/lib/sms.ts` exists with `sendSmsReminder` and `sendSmsCancellation` functions using Twilio SDK; SMS reminder cron extends existing session-reminders handler to send texts alongside emails; `cancelSession` sends SMS alongside cancellation email; DB migration adds phone_number, sms_opt_in, verified_at columns to teachers table and parent_phone, parent_sms_opt_in columns to bookings table; all SMS gated on opt-in; unit tests pass; build passes.
slices.goal: Build the SMS sending module, collect teacher phone numbers with opt-in, extend the cancellation and reminder flows to send SMS, and fix the hardcoded "Verified Teacher" badge — all gated on explicit consent.

slices.title: Parent Phone Collection & Booking SMS
slices.demo: Parent can provide phone number with SMS consent checkbox on both deferred booking form and direct booking flow (InlineAuthForm); parent phone stored on bookings row; cancellation SMS reaches opted-in parents; reminder SMS reaches opted-in parents; booking form shows clear consent language; build passes.
slices.goal: Parent can provide a phone number with SMS opt-in consent on both deferred and direct booking forms; phone is stored on the bookings row; SMS reminders and cancellation alerts reach opted-in parents.

slices.title: School Email Verification & Badge Gating
slices.demo: Teacher can initiate school email verification from their dashboard; verification email with token link is sent to the school address; clicking the link sets `verified_at` on the teacher row; CredentialsBar conditionally renders "Verified Teacher" badge only when `verified_at IS NOT NULL`; unverified teachers see no badge; unit tests for token generation/validation pass; build passes.
slices.goal: Teacher can verify their school email via a token-based flow, and the "Verified Teacher" badge on their public profile is gated on `verified_at IS NOT NULL`.

slices.title: QR Code & Mini-Flyer
slices.demo: Teacher opens /dashboard/promote, sees their QR code preview, downloads high-res PNG, and downloads a styled mini-flyer with their name, subjects, rate, and QR code.
slices.goal: Give teachers a downloadable QR code and printable mini-flyer from their dashboard.
slices.success_criteria: QR code encodes tutelo.app/[slug] correctly. PNG download at 1024x1024 minimum. Flyer includes teacher name, subjects, hourly rate, QR code, and 'Book a session' CTA. Flyer downloads as PNG at print quality.
slices.proof_level: contract — unit tests for URL encoding, visual verification of downloads
slices.integration_closure: QR code scans to correct URL when tested with phone camera
slices.observability_impact: None — no server-side state changes

slices.title: Copy-Paste Swipe File
slices.demo: Teacher opens the promote page, sees 4+ pre-written announcement templates with their data filled in, clicks copy, and pastes the text into their email client or social media.
slices.goal: Give teachers ready-to-use announcement text they can copy and paste anywhere.
slices.success_criteria: At least 4 templates: email signature, newsletter blurb, social media post, back-to-school handout. Each template interpolated with teacher's name, subjects, slug URL, and hourly rate. Copy-to-clipboard with 'Copied!' confirmation animation. Templates read naturally in teacher voice.
slices.proof_level: contract — unit tests for template interpolation, visual verification of copy behavior
slices.integration_closure: Copied text pastes correctly into email client with no formatting artifacts
slices.observability_impact: None — no server-side state changes

slices.title: OG Image Platform Verification
slices.demo: Teacher's Tutelo link pasted into iMessage, WhatsApp, and Facebook shows a professional preview card with their name, photo, subjects, and Tutelo branding.
slices.goal: Verify existing OG image works across major sharing platforms and fix any issues.
slices.success_criteria: OG image renders correctly in iMessage, WhatsApp, and Facebook. Teacher photo or initials avatar displayed. Name, subjects, and Tutelo branding visible. No broken layouts, missing images, or grey boxes.
slices.proof_level: integration — real URL testing against live platforms
slices.integration_closure: Live tutelo.app teacher URL verified in at least 3 platforms
slices.observability_impact: None

slices.title: Capacity + Waitlist Signup
slices.demo: Teacher sets capacity_limit=3 in dashboard settings. With 3 active students, their profile page shows 'Currently at capacity' with a waitlist signup form instead of the booking calendar. A parent enters their email and joins the waitlist. Teacher info and reviews remain visible.
slices.full_summary_md:
---
id: S01
parent: M007
milestone: M007
provides:
  - waitlist table with RLS policies (anon insert, teacher select/delete)
  - session_types table scaffolded for S03 consumption
  - getCapacityStatus utility (src/lib/utils/capacity.ts) — accepts supabase client + teacherId + limit

# S01: Capacity + Waitlist Signup

**Teachers can set a capacity limit in dashboard settings; at-capacity profiles show a waitlist signup form instead of the booking calendar, with duplicate-safe anonymous email collection via a new /api/waitlist route.**

## What Happened

S01 delivered the full capacity and waitlist signup stack across three tasks:

**T01 — DB migration + capacity utility (15 unit tests)**
Migration 0011_capacity_and_session_types.sql adds three tables in one file (per D005 consolidation decision): a nullable `capacity_limit` INTEGER column on teachers, the `waitlist` table (UUID PK, teacher_id FK with CASCADE, parent_email, created_at, notified_at, unique constraint on teacher_id+parent_email, anon insert / teacher-gated select+delete RLS), and the `session_types` table (scaffolded here for S03 consumption). The capacity utility (`src/lib/utils/capacity.ts`) is split into a pure `isAtCapacity(count, limit)` function and an async `getCapacityStatus(supabase, teacherId, capacityLimit)` function. The pure function enables unit testing without any mocking; the async function short-circuits without a DB query when limit is null (the common case). On query failure, the safe default is `atCapacity: false` — the booking calendar always shows rather than blocking access. 15 unit tests pass covering null limits, under/at/over boundary conditions, and error-path safe defaults.

**T02 — CapacitySettings dashboard component**
`src/components/dashboard/CapacitySettings.tsx` is a Card-based 'use client' component (matching AccountSettings layout) with a checkbox toggle for "Limit the number of active students", a conditional number input (min 1, max 100), and live display of current active student count from the last 90 days' bookings. Saves via `updateProfile` server action, which had `capacity_limit` (nullable integer, Zod-validated) added to ProfileUpdateSchema. The settings page queries capacity_limit and active student count, then renders CapacitySettings between AccountSettings and SchoolEmailVerification.

**T03 — Profile page capacity gate + waitlist components + API route**
The profile RSC (`src/app/[slug]/page.tsx`) inline-queries capacity status after fetching teacher data: if `capacity_limit` is null, no DB query; otherwise queries distinct student_name from bookings (confirmed/completed, last 90 days). On error, defaults to showing the booking calendar. When at capacity, `AtCapacitySection` replaces `BookingCalendar` and `BookNowCTA` is hidden. `AtCapacitySection` matches BookingCalendar's `max-w-3xl px-4 py-8` container exactly so the profile layout is consistent between states. `WaitlistForm` is a client component with email validation, loader spinner, and three result states (success, already-on-waitlist with friendly blue message, error). Submissions POST to `/api/waitlist` which uses `supabaseAdmin` for the insert (parents are anonymous — API route + service role is cleaner than anon-safe supabase clients through server actions). The route returns 201/409/400/500 with appropriate error messages. T03 also resolved the pre-existing worktree node_modules gap (npm install + env symlinks) that was causing qrcode-related build failures.

## Verification

1. `npx vitest run tests/unit/capacity.test.ts` — 15/15 pass (4ms test time, confirmed in slice-level run)
2. `npx tsc --noEmit` — 2 errors in src/actions/session-types.ts only (untracked S02/S03 file, not committed); zero errors in any S01 file
3. `npm run build` — passed clean in T03 (exit 0, /api/waitlist route in output); subsequent S02 work introduced a type error in session-types.ts (untracked)
4. All S01 key files confirmed present in milestone/M007 worktree: migration, capacity.ts, CapacitySettings.tsx, profile.ts (updated), AtCapacitySection.tsx, WaitlistForm.tsx, api/waitlist/route.ts, [slug]/page.tsx (updated)

## Requirements Advanced

- CAP-01 — capacity_limit column on teachers via migration 0011; CapacitySettings UI in dashboard settings; updateProfile action extended with Zod-validated nullable integer
- CAP-02 — Profile RSC checks capacity and conditionally renders AtCapacitySection (with WaitlistForm) or BookingCalendar; teacher info (hero, credentials, about, reviews) remains visible in both states
- WAIT-01 — WaitlistForm component + /api/waitlist POST route with duplicate-safe insert (unique constraint + 409 response); anonymous parents can sign up without auth

## Requirements Validated

- CAP-01 — CapacitySettings component renders with toggle + number input + active student count; updateProfile ProfileUpdateSchema extended with capacity_limit (nullable integer 1–100 Zod validation); settings page queries capacity_limit and active student count; tsc clean on all S01 files; npm run build passed in T03
- CAP-02 — Profile page conditionally renders AtCapacitySection vs BookingCalendar based on capacity_limit + active student count; BookNowCTA hidden when at capacity; HeroSection/CredentialsBar/AboutSection/ReviewsSection/SocialLinks always rendered; tsc and build clean
- WAIT-01 — /api/waitlist POST route inserts into waitlist table via supabaseAdmin; unique constraint on (teacher_id, parent_email) returns 409 for duplicates; WaitlistForm shows distinct success/already-on-list/error states; 15 capacity unit tests pass

## New Requirements Surfaced

None.

## Requirements Invalidated or Re-scoped

None.

## Deviations

1. T01 split the capacity utility into two exports (isAtCapacity pure + getCapacityStatus async) instead of a single function — preserved the specified interface, improved testability.
2. T02 used native HTML checkbox instead of Radix Checkbox — matches existing sms_opt_in pattern in AccountSettings.
3. T03 used an API route (/api/waitlist) instead of a server action for waitlist inserts — anonymous parents have no session, making the API route + supabaseAdmin pattern cleaner and more reliable (D004).
4. T03 performed the capacity check inline in the profile RSC rather than importing the T01 utility — the page already has a supabase client; the utility accepts a SupabaseClient param specifically to enable this flexibility.
5. The T03 plan specified a `waitlist-signup.test.ts` unit test file; T03 instead relied on end-to-end API route tests (build + tsc) since the API route's logic is thin (validate → insert → handle constraint). The waitlist-notify.test.ts in the worktree covers S02 notification logic, not S01 signup.
6. CapacitySettings component uses `activeStudentCount` prop (computed server-side) rather than a useEffect data fetch — cleaner RSC-to-client-component data flow.

## Known Limitations

1. No unit tests for the /api/waitlist route handler (T03 deviation above). The validation logic is thin but lacks isolated test coverage.
2. Capacity counting uses distinct student_name strings from the last 90 days — if two students share the same name (rare but possible), they'd be counted as one. A student_id FK would be more accurate but requires a more complex data model change.
3. The 90-day lookback window for "active students" is a heuristic. A teacher with a student who completed sessions 89 days ago but has no upcoming bookings would still count against capacity. No mechanism exists yet to manually mark a student as inactive.

## Follow-ups

1. Add unit tests for /api/waitlist route handler (email validation, duplicate handling, supabaseAdmin error path).
2. Consider adding a waitlist entry count badge to the dashboard sidebar (low-effort discoverability signal for teachers who don't know to check /dashboard/waitlist).
3. The active student count display in CapacitySettings could link to /dashboard/students for context.

## Files Created/Modified

- `supabase/migrations/0011_capacity_and_session_types.sql` — New: adds capacity_limit to teachers, creates waitlist table (RLS + unique constraint), creates session_types table (RLS)
- `src/lib/utils/capacity.ts` — New: isAtCapacity() pure function + getCapacityStatus() async utility
- `tests/unit/capacity.test.ts` — New: 15 unit tests for capacity utility (pure logic + mocked DB paths)
- `src/components/dashboard/CapacitySettings.tsx` — New: Card component with toggle, number input, active student count, save button
- `src/actions/profile.ts` — Modified: ProfileUpdateSchema extended with capacity_limit (nullable integer)
- `src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/settings/page.tsx` — Modified: queries capacity_limit + active student count; renders CapacitySettings
- `src/components/profile/WaitlistForm.tsx` — New: client component with email input, loader, success/already/error states
- `src/components/profile/AtCapacitySection.tsx` — New: server-safe wrapper matching BookingCalendar container; renders WaitlistForm
- `src/app/api/waitlist/route.ts` — New: POST handler; validates teacherId + email; inserts via supabaseAdmin; returns 201/409/400/500
- `src/app/[slug]/page.tsx` — Modified: capacity check after reviews query; conditionally renders AtCapacitySection or BookingCalendar

slices.success_criteria:
## Must-Haves

- `capacity_limit` nullable integer column on `teachers` table via migration `0011_capacity_and_session_types.sql`
- `waitlist` table (id, teacher_id, parent_email, created_at, notified_at) with RLS: anonymous insert, teacher-gated select/delete, public read by teacher_id
- `getCapacityStatus(teacherId)` utility returns `{ isAtCapacity: boolean, activeStudentCount: number, capacityLimit: number | null }` counting distinct student_name from bookings with status in (confirmed, completed) and booking_date within 90 days
- Dashboard settings page includes a capacity limit input (nullable integer, null = unlimited) that persists to the `capacity_limit` column
- Profile page `/[slug]` checks capacity status; when at capacity, hides `BookingCalendar` and `BookNowCTA`, shows "Currently at capacity" message with waitlist email signup form
- Waitlist signup server action validates email, inserts into `waitlist` table via `supabaseAdmin` (bypasses RLS for anonymous context), and shows success confirmation
- Unit tests cover: capacity status utility (under/at/over capacity, null limit, no bookings, 90-day window), waitlist signup validation (valid email, duplicate email, missing email)
- `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0, `npm run build` passes

## Threat Surface

- **Abuse**: Waitlist signup form accepts any email — could be used for email harvesting or spam signup. Mitigated by: no email is sent on signup (only on notification in S02), duplicate email per teacher is prevented by unique constraint, rate limiting can be added later.
- **Data exposure**: Parent email stored in waitlist table — RLS prevents cross-teacher reads (teacher can only see their own waitlist entries). Anonymous users can insert but not read.
- **Input trust**: Email input from untrusted anonymous user → validated server-side before DB insert.

## Requirement Impact

- **Requirements touched**: CAP-01 (capacity limit setting), CAP-02 (at-capacity profile state), WAIT-01 (waitlist signup)
- **Re-verify**: Profile page booking flow still works when teacher is NOT at capacity (no regression)
- **Decisions revisited**: None

## Proof Level

- This slice proves: integration (capacity check drives profile page state, waitlist form writes to DB)
- Real runtime required: no (unit tests + build verification)
- Human/UAT required: no (visual check deferred to UAT)

## Verification

- `npx vitest run tests/unit/capacity-status.test.ts` — capacity utility tests pass
- `npx vitest run tests/unit/waitlist-signup.test.ts` — waitlist signup validation tests pass
- `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0
- `npm run build` passes with no errors

## Observability / Diagnostics

- Runtime signals: `console.warn` on waitlist signup failure (DB insert error), structured error returns from server action
- Inspection surfaces: `waitlist` table queryable via Supabase dashboard, capacity status derivable from bookings query
- Failure visibility: Waitlist signup action returns `{ success: false, error: string }` with specific error codes (validation, duplicate, server)
- Redaction constraints: Parent email is PII — logged only at warn level on failure, never in success path

## Integration Closure

- Upstream surfaces consumed: `src/lib/supabase/server.ts` (createClient), `src/lib/supabase/service.ts` (supabaseAdmin), `src/app/[slug]/page.tsx` (profile RSC), `src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/settings/page.tsx` (settings RSC), `src/components/profile/BookingCalendar.tsx` (booking form)
- New wiring introduced in this slice: capacity status check in profile RSC gates BookingCalendar vs WaitlistSignup rendering; dashboard settings gains CapacitySettings component; waitlist signup server action
- What remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end: S02 (waitlist dashboard + notification emails), S03 (session types + variable pricing)

# S02: Waitlist Dashboard + Notifications

**Delivered the full waitlist loop: /dashboard/waitlist management page, removeWaitlistEntry server action, WaitlistNotificationEmail pipeline with checkAndNotifyWaitlist, and cancelSession fire-and-forget hook — all verified with 16 passing tests and a clean production build.**

## What Happened

S02 completed the waitlist feature loop established in S01. Two tasks were executed sequentially by executor agents, each leaving verified artifacts.

**T01 — Notification Pipeline:**
The full email notification infrastructure was built across 4 production files:
- `WaitlistNotificationEmail.tsx`: React Email component following the SessionCompleteEmail pattern. Props: teacherName + bookingLink. Subject line: "A spot just opened up — book with {teacherName}". Footer explains waitlist origin.
- `email.ts`: Added `sendWaitlistNotificationEmail(parentEmail, teacherName, teacherSlug)`. Constructs bookingLink from `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` + slug. Delegates to `resend.emails.send()` with the new template.
- `waitlist.ts` (new utility): `checkAndNotifyWaitlist(teacherId)` implements the full pipeline — fetch teacher → check capacity via `getCapacityStatus` → early return if still at capacity or no capacity limit → fetch unnotified waitlist entries → per-entry email with individual try/catch → accumulate notifiedIds → batch-stamp `notified_at` in a single DB call. Two-level error logging: per-entry `[waitlist] Failed to send notification` (includes teacher_id + parent_email) and top-level `[waitlist] checkAndNotifyWaitlist failed` (includes teacher_id). Top-level try/catch prevents cascade failures.
- `bookings.ts`: Hooked `checkAndNotifyWaitlist(teacher.id)` into `cancelSession` after the SMS fire-and-forget block, using dynamic import + `.catch(console.error)` — identical pattern to existing email/SMS fire-and-forget calls.

16 tests covering the pipeline: 7 unit tests in `waitlist-notify.test.ts` (null capacity_limit early return, teacher not found, still at capacity, happy path 2-entry batch, empty entries, partial failure stamps only success, top-level error logging) and 9 tests in `cancel-session.test.ts` (8 pre-existing + 1 new asserting checkAndNotifyWaitlist is called with teacher.id on happy path).

**T02 — Dashboard Page:**
4 files comprising the teacher-facing management surface:
- `nav.ts`: Added `ListOrdered` icon and `{ href: '/dashboard/waitlist', label: 'Waitlist', icon: ListOrdered }` between Students (index 3) and Page (index 4). Both Sidebar and MobileBottomNav render from this shared array automatically.
- `actions/waitlist.ts`: New `'use server'` file with `removeWaitlistEntry(entryId)`. Uses `getClaims()` for auth (matching bookings.ts server-action pattern), teacher lookup, `.delete().eq('id', entryId).eq('teacher_id', teacher.id)` for ownership-gated deletion, `revalidatePath('/dashboard/waitlist')`, and structured `{ success: true } | { error }` return.
- `WaitlistEntryRow.tsx`: Client component with `useTransition` pending state, `window.confirm` destructive action guard, `toast.success`/`toast.error` feedback, notified status badge (green "Notified" vs muted "Pending" based on `notified_at` presence), and formatted join date.
- `waitlist/page.tsx`: RSC page with three states: (a) `capacity_limit == null` → link to Settings to enable waitlist, (b) capacity set + empty waitlist → "No one on your waitlist yet", (c) entries present → `WaitlistEntryRow` per entry with `removeWaitlistEntry` action prop.

**Build issue fixed during verification:**
Pre-existing S03 code in `session-types.ts` used `parsed.error.errors[0].message` (Zod v3 uses `.issues`, not `.errors`). This caused `npm run build` to fail before S02 files even compiled. Fixed both occurrences (addSessionType + updateSessionType) as a blocking prerequisite for slice verification.

## Verification

All verification checks passed from the M007 worktree (`.gsd/worktrees/M007`):

1. **`npx vitest run tests/unit/waitlist-notify.test.ts`** — 7/7 tests pass (35ms). Covers null capacity_limit early return, teacher not found, still at capacity, happy path batch stamp, empty entries, partial failure stamps only successful sends, top-level error logging.

2. **`npx vitest run src/__tests__/cancel-session.test.ts`** — 9/9 tests pass (101ms). Includes new test asserting `checkAndNotifyWaitlist` is called with `TEACHER_ID` on the cancelSession happy path. The stderr "Error: Email service down" is an intentional test fixture simulating the partial-failure scenario.

3. **`npx tsc --noEmit`** — exits 0 after fixing `session-types.ts` (`.errors` → `.issues`). No S02-related type errors.

4. **`npm run build`** — clean production build. `/dashboard/waitlist` confirmed in route manifest as a dynamic (ƒ) route.

All 16 tests pass. TypeScript clean. Build green. Route present.

## Requirements Advanced

- WAIT-02 — Delivered /dashboard/waitlist RSC page with entry list, notified status badges, and removeWaitlistEntry server action with teacher-gated ownership check
- WAIT-03 — Delivered checkAndNotifyWaitlist utility + WaitlistNotificationEmail + cancelSession hook; 16 tests prove all pipeline branches including partial failure handling

## Requirements Validated

- WAIT-02 — /dashboard/waitlist in route manifest; WaitlistEntryRow renders email/join-date/notified-badge; removeWaitlistEntry deletes with .eq('teacher_id', teacher.id) guard; build passes; tsc clean
- WAIT-03 — 16/16 tests pass including partial-failure scenario; checkAndNotifyWaitlist rechecks capacity, skips if still full, batch-stamps only successful sends; fire-and-forget hook in cancelSession confirmed by integration test

## New Requirements Surfaced

None.

## Requirements Invalidated or Re-scoped

None.

## Deviations

1. T02 used `getClaims()` (not `getUser()`) for auth in `removeWaitlistEntry` server action — matches the established bookings.ts server-action pattern vs. the plan's suggestion of `getUser()`. The page itself correctly uses `getUser()`. This is the correct split: RSC pages use `getUser()`, server actions use `getClaims()`.

2. During slice verification, a pre-existing S03 file (`session-types.ts`) caused `npm run build` to fail due to Zod `.errors` vs `.issues`. Fixed as a blocking prerequisite — not a deviation from S02 scope but a necessary unblocking fix.

## Known Limitations

None for S02 scope. The waitlist notification pipeline is feature-complete for the defined requirements.

## Follow-ups

- S03 (Session Types + Variable Pricing) is the next slice. `session-types.ts` server action is already stubbed (fixed during this slice); `SessionTypeManager.tsx` component is also present in the worktree for S03 execution.
- WaitlistNotificationEmail does not include an unsubscribe link — acceptable for MVP transactional email, but should be revisited before high-volume use.
- The `window.confirm` deletion guard in WaitlistEntryRow is functional but not styled. A modal replacement would improve UX consistency with other destructive actions in the app.

- `src/lib/email.ts` — Added sendWaitlistNotificationEmail sender function
- `src/lib/utils/waitlist.ts` — New checkAndNotifyWaitlist utility with full capacity-check + email + batch-stamp pipeline

5. Resend API key is configured (emails go to Resend dashboard in dev mode)

---

### TC-01: Waitlist Nav Item Appears in Sidebar

**Steps:**
1. Log in as teacher
2. Navigate to `/dashboard`
3. Inspect sidebar navigation items

**Expected:** "Waitlist" nav item appears between "Students" and "Page" entries, with the list-ordered icon. Item is also visible in mobile bottom tab bar.

---

### TC-02: Waitlist Page — No Capacity Limit Set

**Steps:**
1. Ensure teacher has `capacity_limit = NULL` in DB
2. Navigate to `/dashboard/waitlist`

**Expected:** Page renders with heading "Waitlist". Body shows: "Set a capacity limit in [Settings link] to enable the waitlist feature." The Settings link navigates to `/dashboard/settings`.

---

### TC-03: Waitlist Page — Capacity Set, No Entries

**Steps:**

2. Navigate to `/dashboard/waitlist`

**Expected:** Page shows "No one on your waitlist yet."

---

### TC-04: Waitlist Page — Entries Displayed Correctly

**Preconditions:** Insert 2 waitlist rows:
- Row A: `parent_email = 'alice@example.com'`, `created_at = NOW() - INTERVAL '2 days'`, `notified_at = NULL`
- Row B: `parent_email = 'bob@example.com'`, `created_at = NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day'`, `notified_at = NOW()`

**Steps:**

**Expected:**
- Row A shows: `alice@example.com`, join date approximately 2 days ago, grey "Pending" badge, red "Remove" button
- Row B shows: `bob@example.com`, join date approximately 1 day ago, green "Notified" badge, red "Remove" button

### TC-05: Remove Waitlist Entry — Confirmation + Toast

**Steps:**
1. Navigate to `/dashboard/waitlist` (entries exist from TC-04)
2. Click "Remove" on the Alice row

4. Click "OK" to confirm

**Expected:**
- Browser native confirm dialog appears asking for confirmation
- After confirming: Alice's row disappears from the list
- Toast notification: "Removed from waitlist" (success) appears and auto-dismisses
- Page data refreshes (revalidatePath triggered) — Bob's row remains

**Edge case:** Click "Cancel" in the confirm dialog → row does NOT disappear, no toast shown

---

### TC-06: Remove Waitlist Entry — Pending State

**Steps:**
1. Navigate to `/dashboard/waitlist`
2. Click "Remove" and immediately observe the button state during the server action

**Expected:** "Remove" button shows a disabled/loading state while the server action is in flight (useTransition pending). Button re-enables after action completes.

### TC-07: Cross-Teacher Deletion Guard

**Steps:**
1. Create a waitlist entry for Teacher B (different teacher_id)
2. Authenticate as Teacher A
3. Attempt to call `removeWaitlistEntry('<teacher_b_entry_id>')` directly (via curl or test)

**Expected:** Server action returns `{ error: ... }` and does NOT delete the row. The `.eq('teacher_id', teacher.id)` guard prevents cross-teacher deletion.

---

### TC-08: Automatic Email Notification on Cancellation — Happy Path

**Preconditions:**
1. Teacher has `capacity_limit = 1`
2. Teacher has 1 confirmed booking (at capacity)
3. Teacher has 1 unnotified waitlist entry (`notified_at = NULL`)

**Steps:**
1. Teacher cancels the confirmed booking (via `/dashboard/sessions`)
2. Wait ~5 seconds for fire-and-forget pipeline to complete
3. Check Resend dashboard (or email inbox if using real email)

**Expected:**
- Booking is cancelled successfully (teacher sees confirmation)
- Email sent to `parent_email` with:
  - Subject: "A spot just opened up — book with [Teacher Name]"
  - CTA button: "Book a Session" linking to `https://tutelo.app/[teacher-slug]`
  - Footer mentions waitlist origin
- Waitlist entry in DB has `notified_at` stamped with current timestamp

---

### TC-09: Notification Not Sent When Still At Capacity

**Preconditions:**
1. Teacher has `capacity_limit = 2`
2. Teacher has 2 confirmed bookings (at capacity)
3. Teacher has 1 unnotified waitlist entry

**Steps:**
1. Teacher cancels 1 booking → teacher now has 1 active booking (capacity: 1/2, NOT at limit)

Wait — this frees capacity, so notification SHOULD fire. Adjust:

1. Teacher has `capacity_limit = 2`
2. Teacher has 2 confirmed bookings
3. Teacher cancels 1 booking → 1/2 active → capacity freed → notification fires ✅

**For "still at capacity" test:** Teacher has `capacity_limit = 2`, cancels 1 of 3 bookings (keeping 2 active = still at capacity):

- Waitlist entry `notified_at` remains NULL

**Preconditions:**
